hook: 31 PRs merged this week. We fixed a script that took 25 hours to run, shipped OpenAI-compatible image endpoints, and wired up a ridiculous number of new models.
body:
·˚✿ POLLINATIONS WEEKLY ✿˚·
    vol.42 — speedruns & drop-in endpoints

→ INFRASTRUCTURE SPEEDRUNS
We optimized the Seed tier auto-upgrade workflow. Previously, calculating GitHub activity to upgrade contributors took about 25 hours to complete. It now takes a few seconds. Turns out you don't actually need to bend the space-time continuum to parse a list of commits.

Speaking of time, Pollen credit refills are now hourly instead of daily. If you burn through your local allocation, just go get a coffee and come back.

───

→ OPENAI DROP-IN & NEW MODELS
You can now use our image generation endpoints as a drop-in replacement for OpenAI's API. Change the base URL, keep your existing code, get pixels back.

We also routed a mountain of new models into the infrastructure:
1. Pruna p-image, p-image-edit, and p-video
2. dirtberry and dirtberry-pro
3. claude-airforce via api.airforce
4. qwen3-tts via our new community provider Seraphyn

───

→ BYOP & THE ECOSYSTEM
Bring Your Own Provider (BYOP) auth is becoming the standard. We wired it up for the AI Dungeon Master and Product Packaging apps, alongside a new media upload service. We also fixed a CORS-adjacent blocker that was preventing browser extensions from generating App Keys.

Finally, Polly v2 is live with native-speed internals, and the frontend got a cozy redesign with a new scene background and palette system.
closing: Everything we do is in the open. Repo and roadmap are public at github.com/pollinations
image_prompt: Cozy 8-bit pixel art scene of a retro RPG quest board in a digital garden. The cute bee mascot wearing a tiny sweatband and holding a stopwatch hovers next to the board. A chunky pixel robot with a CRT monitor head is pinning up a quest notice. Large pixel font on the wooden board reads 'WEEKLY WRAP'. Smaller pixel notes pinned to the board read '25H -> 2S', 'OPENAI API', and 'NEW MODELS'. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender pastel gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
image_text: WEEKLY WRAP / 25H -> 2S / OPENAI API / NEW MODELS
reasoning: The 25h -> seconds optimization is a perfect hook for developers who appreciate the absurdity of software. Paired with the highly requested OpenAI compatibility and hourly pollen refills, it makes for a strong, dense technical update.

    vol.42 — speedruns & drop-in endpoints

Speaking of time, Pollen credit refills are now hourly instead of daily. If you burn through your local allocation, just go get a coffee and come back.

───
